question: This area chart shows average housing prices ($1000s) across different cities. What is the most accurate statement according to the chart?

options.A: The chart incorrectly applies a continuous filled area over unrelated categories, creating a false impression of a smooth trend between them.
options.B: Denver clearly has the highest price among all categories.
options.C: The chart shows a generally increasing or decreasing pattern across the categories, indicating an underlying continuous progression.
options.D: New York clearly has the lowest price among all categories.

explanation: This area chart joins category values for city into a continuous filled region. However, the x‑axis categories (e.g., New York, Austin, Nashville, San Francisco, ...) have no inherent numeric or spatial continuity, so the apparent smooth trend is an artifact of the chart type rather than a meaningful pattern. A bar chart would allow readers to compare each category independently without implying a continuous progression.
